message:
feat(conformance): add virtual_memory_ops executors (bd-dc02)

Add execute_* handlers for virtual_memory_ops family:
- mmap: calls ABI with cleanup, handles zero length case
- munmap, mprotect, madvise: stub (need valid mapped addresses)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-cx4","title":"Kernel: Blackwell approachability controller (design safe set + payoff vector)","description":"Design a Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective routing.

Goal:
- Guarantee that the cumulative payoff vector (latency, risk, coverage, etc.) approaches a safe set under the chosen policy.

Design tasks:
- Define payoff vector components using existing cached signals.
- Define target safe set (convex polytope) per mode.
- Define arms/actions (Fast vs Full; Repair vs Deny thresholds).
- Choose update rule that is O(1) and integer-friendly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Written derivation of approachability update + how it improves over ad-hoc thresholds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:08.326514210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:26:24.499424179Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:26:24.499403230Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1080,"issue_id":"bd-cx4","author":"PinkMill","text":"Design complete. Written to approachability_design.md. 3D payoff (latency,risk,coverage), box safe set, 4 arms, integer-only O(1) update, O(1/sqrt(t)) convergence (Blackwell 1956). Legacy anchor: malloc/nptl.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:26:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-d5l","title":"Perf: Microbench RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe (criterion + ns/op)","description":"Create a repeatable benchmark that measures runtime_math overhead directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bench measures decide() and observe_validation_result() separately and together.
- Reports p50/p95/p99 ns/op and throughput.
- Produces stable output suitable for regression gating.

Implementation notes:
- Keep benchmark deterministic: fixed inputs, fixed warmup, pin thread if needed.
- Include both strict and hardened mode paths.
- Record compiler flags / CPU model in output metadata.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.703138634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254231420Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254213887Z","close_reason":"bench implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1081,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"Implemented criterion microbench  (decide / observe_validation_result / decide+observe) in  and wired it in .

Output:
- Prints  (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, /proc/cpuinfo model).
- Prints  per benchmark.

Determinism knobs:
- Fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx).
- Fixed warmup loops (10k iters) before measurement.
- Optional Linux CPU pinning via .","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:09:18Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"NOTE: Previous comment accidentally expanded benchmark output due to shell backticks; ignore it for instructions.

What changed:
- Added crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_bench.rs
- Added [[bench]] runtime_math_bench entry in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml

Bench targets:
- runtime_math/decide/<mode>
- runtime_math/observe_fast/<mode>
- runtime_math/decide_observe/<mode>

How to run:
- Strict: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=strict cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench
- Hardened: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=hardened cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench

Output:
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META lines (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, CPU model)
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH lines with p50/p95/p99/mean ns/op and throughput ops/s

Determinism:
- fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx)
- fixed warmup loops (10k iters)
- optional Linux CPU pinning: GLIBC_RUST_BENCH_PIN=1","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:05Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-dfe","title":"EPIC: Inventory + drift control (AGENTS.md vs runtime_math code; snapshot schema stability)","description":"Goal: prevent architectural drift.

Why:
- runtime_math has many moving parts; if AGENTS.md lists required modules/decision law but code diverges, future work becomes inconsistent.

Outputs:
- A repeatable check that module inventory + decision law in AGENTS.md matches code.
- A stable, versioned RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema (new fields are additive and documented).
- Fusion signal vector size always matches number of cached states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.669320398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43.636260651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:36.812282675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1az","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-29r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-31i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-ju7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-p5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesn’t silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: “why did runtime choose FullValidate here?” with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module can’t land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:17Z"},{"id":1084,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 7 blockers CLOSED. check_module_inventory.sh (68/68 in sync), check_module_wiring.sh, check_snapshot_coverage.sh (68/68 covered), snapshot_gate.sh, perf_gate.sh all passing. Schema versioning, cadence classification, fusion consistency, decision-law validation all delivered.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-di5w","title":"Track A: Implement _IO_iter_* native stream iteration over NativeStreamRegistry","description":"## Goal
Replace 4 stub _IO_iter_* functions in io_internal_abi.rs (lines 1430-1457) with real implementations that iterate over the NativeStreamRegistry.

## Current State
All 4 functions return null, making the stream list appear empty:
- _IO_iter_begin() -> null (should return first occupied slot index)
- _IO_iter_end() -> null (should return sentinel past last slot)
- _IO_iter_file(iter) -> null (should return FILE* for the slot)
- _IO_iter_next(iter) -> null (should advance to next occupied slot)

## Why This Matters
glibc atexit/fflush-all path may walk _IO_list via these iterators. If the list is empty, exit-time flushing of native streams is silently skipped by any code using glibc's _IO_iter API. Our native fflush(NULL) works via NativeStreamRegistry.flush_all(), but external code (atexit handlers in host glibc) that walks _IO_list via iterators would miss our streams.

## Design
Use slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding:
- _IO_iter_begin: Lock registry, find first OCCUPIED slot (index 0..255), return (index+1) cast to *mut c_void. Return null if no occupied slots. We use index+1 so that 0 (stdin at slot 0) is distinguishable from null.
- _IO_iter_end: Return (STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY+1) cast to *mut c_void as sentinel. This is 257, always > any valid encoded slot.
- _IO_iter_file: Decode (iter as usize - 1) to get slot index, call registry.get(index), return &NativeFile as *mut c_void. Return null if index is invalid or slot is empty.
- _IO_iter_next: Decode current slot index from iter, scan forward for next OCCUPIED slot, return encoded index or end sentinel.

### Thread Safety
The registry is behind a Mutex. Each iterator call acquires and releases the lock independently. This means the iteration is NOT atomic — streams could be added/removed between calls. This matches glibc behavior (glibc's _IO_iter functions are also not atomic without _IO_list_all_lock held externally).

### Implementation Helpers
Add to NativeStreamRegistry:
- first_occupied() -> Option<usize>: scan slots[0..] for first SLOT_OCCUPIED
- next_occupied(after: usize) -> Option<usize>: scan slots[after+1..] for next SLOT_OCCUPIED

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs: Replace 4 function bodies, add 2 helpers to NativeStreamRegistry

## Unit Tests (in tests/io_internal_native_file_test.rs)
- test iter_begin_returns_first_stream: Register 0 extra streams -> begin iterates over stdin/stdout/stderr (3 entries)
- test iter_begin_end_empty_after_unregister: Unregister all stdio, begin == end (no streams)
- test iter_file_returns_valid_native_file: iter_file on valid iterator returns non-null with correct fd
- test iter_file_on_end_returns_null: iter_file on end sentinel returns null
- test iter_next_advances_to_next_occupied: skip empty slots correctly
- test iter_next_at_end_returns_end: advancing past last stream returns end sentinel
- test iter_full_traversal_count: register 5 streams, iterate begin->next->next...->end, count matches open_count()
- test iter_survives_concurrent_unregister: unregister a stream during iteration, no crash

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_io_iter_native.sh
Build and run a focused Rust test binary that:
1. Opens 10 files via our native fopen (if available) or memfd_create
2. Walks _IO_iter_begin -> _IO_iter_next until _IO_iter_end
3. Counts entries and verifies >= 10
4. Emits structured JSONL trace per step

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=io_internal, symbol=_IO_iter_begin|end|file|next, slot_index, result_ptr, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 _IO_iter_* functions return real iterators over NativeStreamRegistry
- 8+ unit tests pass covering positive, negative, edge, and concurrency cases
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:17.216902967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935547570Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935252511Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_iter_begin/end/file/next over NativeStreamRegistry using slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding. Added first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers. 8 new integration tests: begin returns first stream, end is constant sentinel, file returns valid NativeFile, file on end/null returns null, next past end stays at end, full traversal visits all streams, traversal after unregister skips freed slots. All 40 io_internal tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io-internal","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1361,"issue_id":"bd-di5w","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implementation note: NativeStreamRegistry already has get() and open_count(). Use slot index as opaque pointer (usize cast to *mut c_void). Sentinel for _IO_iter_end = STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY (256). Lock registry mutex during scan. Consider exposing first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers on NativeStreamRegistry.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ef2","title":"I/O migration wave: route core unistd/io ABI entrypoints through raw veneer","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Migrate open/close/read/write/pread64/pwrite64/lseek/fcntl subset used by smoke suite.
- Preserve strict mode ABI semantics and hardened mode evidence emission.

Acceptance:
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite passes with migration enabled.
- Compatibility fixtures show no semantic drift on return values/errno.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.198369909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","io","syscall"]}

 {"id":"bd-zh1y.7.2","title":"Implement minimal UDP DNS stub resolver (A/AAAA records)","description":"Parse /etc/resolv.conf for nameserver IPs. Construct DNS query packets (RFC 1035) for A and AAAA record types. Send via UDP socket to nameserver, parse response. Handle: CNAME indirection (1 level), timeout/retry (2 attempts, 5s timeout), search domain suffix from resolv.conf. This is ~200-300 lines of code — musl's approach is a good reference. Target: frankenlibc_core::resolv::dns module.","acceptance_criteria":"DNS stub sends well-formed A/AAAA queries via UDP. Parses responses correctly. Handles CNAME (1 level). Retries on timeout.
